title: Ordinance for Doctoral Programmes-SPA Bhopal
text: I School of Planning and Architecture, Bhopal Ordinance for Doctoral Programmes (Amended) 2018 (Approved in 34 neetiog of BOG held on f June,2018) -- 1 of 34 -- st. Functions of Scholar Research Review Committee (SRRC) 20 Constituton of Ooctora, Advisory Committee (DAC) 20 -21 26 21 Contents Contents Functions of Doctoral Advisory Committee Review of the Research 21 -22 -- 2 of 34 -- 22Acceptance of Employment - Temporary Relief from Ph.D Progl.amme 2a 22 -23 Duraton of PhD Programme 29. 23 -24 Pre -Thesis - Submission Seminar 30 24 Publication of Research Work 24 -27 Submission of Dissertation Report 32. 27 -29 Appointment of Extemal Examiners and Extemal Evaluation of Disse(ation Report 33. 2e -29 Final Defense of Dissertation Report and Award ol PhD Degree 34 29-30 Leave Admissible for a FullTime PhD Scholar 35. 31 Legal and Other [4atters 36. -- 3 of 34 -- Ordinance for Doctoral programmes (Amended) 2O1g Preamble Scfrool of Planning and Architocture Bhopat (SPAB), established in 2OO8 under an Act of Parliament as an autonomous institute o, nationat importance by the Minist y of Human Resource Development Government of lndia is mandaied to promote education anct research in planning and architecfural studies. Given ib mandate, SPAB intends to t€ the preeminent pubtic research and teaching academy in architecture and planning in the country and aspires to attain (a) international standard by integreting teaching and learning, (b) advancement of the knowiedge base through research and scholarship and (c) leadership in seMce and out each. SPAB aspires to emerge as an intemational academy whose academic portfolio, research ag6nda, and educational model atign with the shiiing needs of the society. It stives for built sustenance through sustainable architecture, social sustenan@ through universal design, culfural sustenance through conservation, and human setdement sustenance th.ough habitat ptanning and design. The school is committed to train planners and architecb for the nation who could exhibit high academic strandards and take up the challenges of physical and socio- environmental development of lhe couniay. SPAB aspires to engage in meaningful research on topical themes in the areas which inter alia include sustainable ar.fiitecture, urban planning, heritage @nservation, urban design, universal design, regional planning, Eansport and logistics planning, landscape architecture and design. SPAB shall make allendeavours to: . promote a cadre of high caliber faculty devoted to teaching, research and consultancy in all disciplines pe.taining to planning and Architecture. . create a national repository of data base and decision support cenlre for the preparation and implementation of various habitat development programmes. . emerge as a socially responsible institution providing research feedback to the Govemment for holislic development of human setflements through Arcfritectural and Planning interventions Aryroved in 34 AOG 'llPage -- 4 of 34 -- Wth the above aim and ob.iectives, SPAB offers doctoral programme leading to the award of Ph.D. degree. The broad objectives of the Ph.D. prcg.amme ate not only to keep pace with the expanding horizons of knowledge, but also to contribute to the present needs of the society in relation to social, cultural and environmental sustenance through the research findings. The Ph.D. programme at SPAB intends to encourage analylical investigations into the areas of research applying qualitative or quantitative approach with the ultimate objective of culminating into a creative and productive enquiry into the domain of Architecture and Pianning. The academic programme leading to lhe Ph.D. degree is broad-based and involves a minimum course credit requiremenl and a research dissertation. SPAB sbongly encourages research ln interdisciplinary areas through a well coordinated interdepa(mental academic collaboration. The presence of a dedicated pool of tuculty in SPAB provides excellent opportunities ior carrying out research in divetse fields of Architecture and Planning. Definitions 'Ph D. scholar' shall mean an indiMdual registered for the Ph.D ramme in SPAB 'Senale'shall mean Senate of School of Planning and A.chitectu.e, Bhopal 'DRC' shall mean Departmental Research Committee 'SRRC' shall mean Scholar Research Review Committee v 'DAC' shall mean Doctoral Advisory CommitEe vl 'DoAA'shall mean Dean of Academic Afiai6 tx. 'AR (A)' shall mean Assistant Registrar (Academi6) x. 'RG' shall mean Regulation Ordinance a) SPAB awards the PhD in various specializations under Architecture and Planning to a registered PhD scholar who has successtully Programme. Ietod the PhD Appoved in 3{ BOG 2lPage -- 5 of 34 -- b) The PhD Programme with the goveming regulations is formulated by the Senate of SPAB. The Senate can modiry regulatjons from time to time as and when wananted. c) A PhD scholar to be awarded the phD degree has to submit a dissertation report incorporating the findings of his./her research canied out under this programme_ The dissertation shoutd make an originat conlfbution of high quality to tne advancement of knowledge as judged by the experts in the relevant area. d) A PhD scholar becomes eligibte for the award of the phD degree after fulfilling allthe academic requirements prescribed by the Senate. e) The award shall be made upon the recommendation of lhe Senate of SPAE! and its ratification by the Board of Govemors of SPAB. 0 The PhD degree shall b€ awarded in the discipline ot the Department which registers the PhD scholar for the phD programme. Requlalions The degree of Doctor of philosophy, abbreviated as ph.D., shall be awarded to a PhD scholar upon the complelion of hjs / her writing the dissertation which exhibits a high quality criticat and analytical investigation into the assigned area of research that leads to original cont.ibutjon to the exisling knowledge. The phD degree of SPAB shall be conlerred on a phD scholar wtro tulfits a the requiremenb specified in the Regulat'ons (RG). The phD programme of SPAB wifl be coordinated by the Dean, Academic Affairs (DoAA) appointed by the Chairperson, Senate from amongst the faculty of SPAB wilh ph.D., lyho has cohsiderable research experience and successtully guided at least one ph-D. schotar. DoAA shall be assisted by a Coordinator to be nominated by the Chairperson, Senate from amongst any regular SPAB faculty having a doctoral degree. The Coordinator of Doctoral programme shall assist the DoAA to conduct the phD programme smoothly. tion 1: Ad ion to PhD Pmo ramme a) DoAA shall issue the Admission Notice for the phD programme offered under different departments of SpAE]. b) An applicant shall appty in prqscnbed format (Annexure l) gadmission to the Awovedin3{Boc 3tpase W- -- 6 of 34 -- PhD Programme within the stipulated time mentioned in the Admission Notification made under RG '1 (a). c) DoAA thall constitute lnteMew Panel (s) with prior approval ot lhe Chairpetson, Senate to oversee the admission process to the PhD programme. d) An applicant shall indicate his / her choice of department under which s/he wishes to carry out his / her PhD research. However the DAC reseNes the right to allocab a department to a PhD scholar other than lhe department s/he opts for based on his/her proposed area of research. e) The Admission to rle PhD programme shall usually be made at the beginning of each Academic session with the provision of having bi - annual admission subject to the number of applicetions reoeived, number of vacant seats available with prior permission ofthe Chairperson, Senate. D Admission to the PhO progElmme shall be conduc{ed through an enbance test by the Admassion Committee to be constituted by DoAA with prior approval of the Chairperson, Senate. The enbance test shall have two parts: Part - A carrying fifty ma*s shall be a written test common to all applicants to judge the analytical ability and knowledge of the concerned Core Subiec't of the candidate. Part - B carrying fifly marks shall be a viva voce conducted by the respective lnterview Panels on the basis of a short PowerPoint presentation by the candidate on his / her area of research interest' The Admission Committee may relax the criteria of in-person appearance at lhe entrance test and viva voce aM instead conduct an interview through Skype in the case of Foreign Nationals and highly qualified and vastly experienced candidates. g) Ofrce of AR (A) shall verify the documents before issuing an Admit Card to the applicantto sit for the enkance tesl h) AR (A) shall cause to send the malks scored by ttre applicants in the wriften part of the entrance test to the office of DoAA soon after the evaluation of answer scripts. DoAA shall send the marks sh€et to th€ respective lnterview Panels for conduclng the viva voce part of the entrance test. i) The format oi entrance test may be altered by the Admission Committee as and when warrarted subject to the approval of Chairpe6on, awroved in 3f BoG 4lPage -- 7 of 34 -- j) Admission of an appticar{ to the phD programme sha,l be made after due consideration to written test, the uflity of the research area indjcated by the applicant at the viva voce. consiatenfly good academic pedormance, sclre in MHRD recognized schotarship(s), availabitity of qualified faculg at SPAB to superyise the research, availability of required infrastructure at SPAE! and extent of external assistance required. k) Upon qualifying himself/hers€lf at the enlrance test, an applicant shall be provisionally admitted to the phD programme on payment of prescribed fees and shall be registered under the Department preferably as per the choice of the app,icant if nol otheMise decided by the DAC. l) Once admited to the phD programme under .espective Department, the respeclive DRC shall facilitate and review th6 research wo* of the phD scholar. m) Although, it is not plausible to have identical number of phD scholars across the Departments, allocation of phD scho{ars across each Department sha depend on the availabjlity of Supervisor in each department and taking into consideration the maximum number of phD scholars a pemanent fulltime Iaculty of SPAB in that department can supervise / co_supervise. n) Selected applicants \,yho are in employment elsewhere must produce a No Objection Certificate from their respeclive institute / employer at the time of admission to the phD programme. o) Reservalions for SC/ ST/ OBC/ persons with Disabilities and other categorjes shall be as per Govemment of lndia norms. p) A PhD scholar shall normally be admitted to the phD programme in the odd semesler- However, a waiflisted phD scholar may be admitted to the phD programme in the even semester as well subject to vacancy of seats. q) The total number ol seats under the phD programme shall be revised by the Senate froft time to time and the admission to the phD programme shall be restricted to the max,mum numbea of seats so apprcved. r) An applacant registered to the phD programme shall continue to be a phD scholar until the otficial notification towards the contument of phD dogree to him/her Apwved in 34 BOG 5lPage -- 8 of 34 -- Reoulation 2: Edqcauonaland Professional Eliqibilitv for Admission to PhD Proqramme An applicant shall be eligible to apply for the Ph.D. programme of SPAB provided Jhe 1. (a) has qualified for the award oI Masters degree of a recognized institution or university in various specialization of Architecture or Planning or allied subjects with 60% marks (55% for reserved categories) or equivalent grade. OR 2. (a) has qual'Iied for the award of Bachelors degree of a recognized institution or university in Architecture or Planning wlth 75% marks O0% for reserved categories) or equivalent grade. (b) has professional / research expeience for a minimum period of five years after obtaining the Bachelots degree. Requlation 3: Cateoories of Admissions Applicants wjll be admitted to the Ph.D. programme of SPAB under any one of the following categories: a) Full-time PhD scholars with MHRD Scholarship,/ Fellowships. b) Full-time PhD scholars under the Quality lmprovement Programme (OIP) with fellowships. c) Full time PhD schola6 with other Govemment / Quasi Government / lnstitutional Fellowship or Scholarship d) self-fnanced lndian (inoluding ocl, Plos) and Foreign tull time or part time PhD scholars. e) Full time / part time PhD scholar under international collaboration including tccR. D Part time PhD scholar who is a Permanent Facuity / Research Assistant ' Technical Assistant or any other duly approved category of SPAB stafi and has the minimum educa0onal qualifications as prescribed under RG 2 and has an e)eerien@ of more than 6ve Years. g) Part time PhD scholar who is attached to any SPAB researcl2r consultancy Approvodin3i Boc 6tPa s e W* -- 9 of 34 -- projec-t. An applicant admitted to the phD programme under this category must submit his/her apptication form through the Dean (R&D) of SPAB. h) Full time or part time phD scholars sponsored by any institution / organizalion recognized by SPAB Such spohsored candidate must have at least five yearc of professional / research expeaience and must submit his/her application through his/her emptoyer with suitable endorsement that srfhe sha:l be granted leave for the mandatory residency period required under the phD programme i) Full time PhD scholars registered earlier in other UGC recognized institutions / universities and transferred to SPAB as per UGC norms. j) lnespective of the award of any Scholarship / Fellowship / Sponsorship, an applicant must have the minimum educational quatification and expedence as prescribed under RG 2 and must qualify at the enbance test conducbd under RG 1 (D to be eligibte for admission to the phD programme. k) A tull time PhD schotar may apply in prescribed format (Annexure) to ca.ry out the rcsearch as part time phD schotar to DoAA only after the successful completion of coursework and topic fnalization. S/he shall cease to draw any fellorrship / scholarship fiom the date of his / her application. l) No PhD scholar can revert to tu time once his / her application has been accepted by DoAA except under exLa ordinary circumstiances as approved by the Chairperson, Senate. Requlations 4: lntemational Students Foreign nationals can register as full time phD scholars. Foreign Nationats with degree from lndian Lrniversities will be treeted at par with lndian nationals for admission purpose and their eligibitity would be as per RG 2. Foreign National8 with ,oreign degrees must meet the minimum educational requirements as given in RG 2. SPAB raserves the right to ask for vatid scores in GATE / CEED / cRE or equivalent and TOEFU IELTS/ pTE, if warranted. lfiespective of his / her obtaining the degree from either lndian / Foreign university, a foreign national must qualify at the entrance test as given under RG 1 (f). Requlation 5: Dailv Attendance and Guidelines for Availinq Scholarship /Fell hio a) A full time phD scholar shall record his / he, attendance daily in the awoved in 34 Boc TlPage -- 10 of 34 -- Attendance Register available with the ofilce of DoAA dudng the entire period of his./ her studentship as a fulltime PhD scholar. b) A part time PhO scholar shall record his / her attendance daily in the Attendance Register available with the office of DoAA till the mandatory residency Period. c) A PhD scholar admitted to the PhD programme under any scholarship / fellowship / sponsorship must produce the valid award letter issued by he respective scholarship / fellowship / sponsorship agency d) No PhD scholar shall be p€tmitH to receive any emolumenta, salary, stipend or any other scholarship / assistanbhip during the tenure of any scholarship / fellowship / sponsorship under which s/he has been admitted to lhe PhD programme. e) SPAB reserves the right to grant the Scholarship / Fellowship under GATE, JRF etc. to a PhD scholar provided s/he possesses a score lhat is above the cut off level as decided by the competent authority from time to time 0 Continuance of the scholarship / fellowship / sponsorship shall be contingent upon the progress of the research made by the respective PhD scholar as evaluated by the respective DRC and shall be withdrawn if so wa.ranted on grounds of poor or no progress. g) No PhD scholar shall normally be allowed to change the category under which s/he is enrolled to the PhD programme. Reoulation 6: Nature of Work aqainst Schola.shiD, FellolYship Full-Time PhD scholaG getting Scholarship / Fellowship shall be required to assist in the following activities assigned by DoAA: a) Assisting in the research work / Projects of SPAB b) Assisting in a laboratory course (not more than one laboratory oouBe at the bachelors or masters level in a semester) c) Assisting in the tutorial / studio (no more than the tutorial of one theory @urse at the bachelors or masters level in a semester) d) Assisting in the maintenance of SPAB website e) Assisting in the maintenance of SPAE) library 0 Assisting in the maintenanoe of SPAB laboratories Apprcved h 3{ BoG SlPage -- 11 of 34 -- g) Assisting in academic and other events of SPAB, CoA / lTpl visits The Work Schedute wil be intimated to the phD schotars through the office of DoAA at the beginning of every semester. tn no case. the work load should exceed eight hours per week. The phD schotars shall not be assigned a complete theory course to teach at the bachelors or masters level. preferably they may be attached only to tutorials/ studios. The phD scholaE sha not be involved in settirg of question papers and evaluatton of answer scripts of bachelors or masteB courses. During the leave of absence of a phD scholar, as admissible under RG 29. the work assigned to him/hershall be re-allotted to other phD s.holars, if need be. Requlation 7: Reqistration a) An applicant having qualified h;mself / herself at fte entrance test shall be admitted to the phD programme upon the payment of requisite fees. b) A PhD scholar shall renew his/her registration for each academic year by paying the requisite iees till the academic year during which she defends her disseftation report in the viva voce. c) A PhD schotar shall confrm the amount of tues payable from the offce of AR (A) before tendering it. d) A PhD schotar shall pay the prescribed Examination Fees at the time ol submitting the dissertation report. e) Failure to renew registration for subsequent academic year for two consecutive academic years shall lead to the cancellation of registration from the PhD programme. 0 Foreign PhD schotar shal pay equivatent amount of all prescribed fees from time to tjme in lndian rupees. Requlalion 8: Cancellation of phD Reqistration Registralion of a PhD scholar shall stand cancelled for any of the followjng reasons: a) Giving false information at the time of applicalion / admassion. b) Not conforming to the regulations ofthe phD programme. c) Failure to successtu,ly complele the prescribed cou.sework. d) Consistent lack of progress in research. e) Violation of discjpline and conduct rules of SPAB, D Not submitting the dissertation report rlithin the stiputated period Appbved in 3? Bac 9lPage -- 12 of 34 -- g) Submission of plagiarized report h) Non submission of progress repo.t to DRC at least a fortnight before ihe DRC meets ,or three consecutive occasions. i) Not enrolling for academic session / semester by paying prescribed tues within the slipulated dates. j) Rejection of dissertation report by extemal examiners. k) Failure to defend (ne viva voce satisfactorily. l) Remaining absent beyond the petiod as granted under RG 29. Requlation 9: Conferment ot PhD Deqree The Ph.D- degree shall be confened to a registered PhD scholar subject to the fulfi llment of following conditions: a) Successtul compleEon of the coursework within the prescribed residency period as given under RG 1 0 and RG 'l '1 . b) Finalizatjon of dissertatjon topic within the prescribed period from the date ot registration to the PhD Programme as given under RG '12. c) Successtully carrying out the researoh under the guidance of approved supeMsor(s) for at least two years from the date of topic finalization' d) Publication / presentation of paper in peer reviewed iournals and seminars I conferences respectively as given under RG 25. e) Satjsfactory performance at the Pre-Thesis€ubmission seminar as given under RG 24. f) Affirmative concurrence to the dissertaton report by the external examiners as given under RG 27. g) Successful defense of the dissertation at the viva voce as given under RG 2E' Requlation 10; conduclins the Coursework a) Respective DRC shall Propose an appropriate coursework for the PhD scholar taking into oonsideration the PhD scholar's intended area of research immediately after the registtation of the PhD scholar- b) The coursework shall comprise ol three subjects and two seminar presentations. Vvhile one subiect will be Research Mgthodology to be conducted by the omce of DoAA, two other subjecb will be from theory sub,ects to be chosen from either the compulsory or eleciivfubjecb ofiered Apptovd, in 34 Boa lolPa s e W- -- 13 of 34 -- by respective departments at the Maters level. A[ the three Subjects and Seminar presenlations shall be awaded grade / marks. c) Respective DRC shall forward the recommended subjects for a phD scholar to the concerned Head of Departnent (s) through lhe office of DoAA. d) Coordinator of Doctoral programme shall ensure that the concerned phD scholaris enrolled to the particularclass on the subject. e) The evaluation oI transfer of knowledge against the two theory Subjects and he Subiect on Research Methodology (as given under RG 10 b) shall be decided by ffte respective Subject Coordinator and / or the DRC / Committee set up by the Chairperson of Senate- D Coordinator of Doctroral programme shall maintain a Register for entering the Subjects succ€sstully completed by the respective phD scholar. g) A PhD schotar wi be required to make two semina. presentations before the concerned DRC on the lopics approved by the latter as part of his / her coursework. A phD schotar shall have b submit a write up on the seminar topic within 3000 - SOOO words with a proper tite, objectives, literature survey, methodology, lindings / obseryatjons and a brief bibliography. h) A PhD scholar shafl present a po\,,/erpoint presenhtion on approved topics in both Seminar 1 and 2. Ihe powerpoint presentation should idea y be restricted to twenty slides. The powerpoint presentation shall be completed within trrirty minutes following which there would be a twenty minute question - answer s€ssion_ i) Distribution of credit aqoss the coursewor* would be as foltows: N.B- Credit{s) for Subiect l ard 2are *bject to changes from lime to dme by the respeclive departm€nt D A PhO schotar may take all the three Subjects or any one su bject (as Coursework Credit Remarks Subject 1 Any theory subiect offered at masters level by any de Subject 2 3 eory subject ofiered at mastgrs level by any ttr Subject 3 Research Methodology Seminar 'l 3 Seminar 2 3 within the given timB To be with resented p the tn time glven To be period presented Apptoved in 3{ BOG lrlPaqe -- 14 of 34 -- mentioned under RG 10 i) offeied by any other institute (recognized by SPAB) subjeot to the approval of concemed DRC- k) The Subiect - part of the coursework (Subject 1, 2 and 3) must be completed within a year (i.e., within two consecutive semeslers) ftom the date of registration to the PhD programme. l) A PhD scholar shall normally present only one seminar in eaoh semester subject to the condition that s/he shall not be allowed to present any seminar in the first semesbr. A PhD scholar however must complete presenting his / her bolh seminars within a matmum period of two years (ie., within four consecutive semesters) from the date of registation to the PhD programme. m) Registration of a PhD schotar, availing semester drop, shall stand cancelled if s/he fails to complete the @ursework within three years (i'e., within six consecutive semesters) from the date of registration to the PhD programme' Reoulation 1l: Requirement of Minimum Residencv Period and Semester Droo {Withdrawal Perlod) a) The minimum residency period for successfully completing the prescribed coursework for a Ph.D. schotar shall be twelve months from the date of registration to the PhD programme. b) The minimum residency period shall not apPly in the case of those PhD scholars who choose to study all he ihree Subjecb as mentioned under RG 10 (i) otrered by any other instituie (recognized by SPAB) subjeGl to the approval of concemed DRC. c) A PhD scholar will not be allowed to withdraw iemporarily from ttle PhD programme before the comple8on of the coursewqrk. d) A PhD scholar may be granted sem6ter drop upto a maximum of two semestgrs on bonafide grounds by the Chairperson, Senate. Except on medical gaounds or ext? ordinary circumstances' semester drop !s not permissible before successful completion of coursework and finalization of topic within the prescdb€d period. A PhD scholar applying for semester d'op shall apply in prescribed format (Annexure) and submit his / her application to the Chairperson of respective DRC through his/her Supervisor. The DRC shall examino the gravity of the case and send its observation DoAA who will Apptowd in 34 BoG 12lPa ge -- 15 of 34 -- forward the same to the Chairpe6on, Senate with his/her observation. Casqs of semester drop shall be reported to the Senab and no scholarship / fellowship shall be admissible during the period of a semester drop. The pedod of semester drcp will not be counted ;n the prescribed time limit for completion of the phD programme. e) After the minimum residency period as prescribed in RG 11 (a), a ph.D. scholar shall be permitted to carry out pan or whole of hjs/ her research work outside SPAB in any laboratory, workshop, worksites and other research centres recognized by SPAB. Requlation 12: Finalization ofthe Dissertation Tooic a) The PhD scholar in consuttalion with his / her Supervisor shall finalize the dissertation topic. b) Once the PhD scholar and his / her supeMsor agrce with ttle topic, the Supervisor shall request the concerned DRC to examine it. DRC shallconduct the Topic - finatization seminar_ Chairperson of concemed DRC shall ensure the parlicipation ol at least one external expert of the DRC to the topic finalization seminar. c) The PhD scholar shall submit a synopsis on the proposed topic o, dissertation and give a Powerpoint presentation before the DRC. d) The synopsis shoutd be typed within a rninimum of SOOO or a maximum of 10000 words in Ariel, 1.1 font..t.5linespacilg. e) The Pori/ePoint presentation should ideally not have more than twenty stides and should be compteted within thirty minutes. The presentation shall be followed by thirty minute question - answer session between the phD scholar and the DRC. 0 Upon satisfying itself with lhe proposed dissertation topic, fle concemed DRC shall recommend approval to the topic to the Chairpe6on, Senate through DoAA g) DoAA shall rectmmend approval to the topic in prescribed format (Annexure) to the Chairperson, Senate for app,oval of Senate. DoAA may seek DAC,S endorsement to the proposed topic, if wananbd, before recommending it to the Chairperson, Senate for approval of Senate. h) DoAA may atso seek DAC,S cnrtorsement " "" *ffiI for additionat Awovedin3r Boc l3rpase (//.I| ,w -- 16 of 34 -- SupeNisor from the concerned DRC and shall recommend the same lo the Chairperson, Senate if addltional supeNision is found necessary' i) On receipt of approval from the Chairperson, Senate' DoAA shall inform the DRC and &re PhD scholar and the latter shall proceed with lhe research work on receipt of such communication liom DoAA in presqibed format (Annexure). j) ln case the Senate does not meet within two monlhs of the submission of topic fnalization recommendation by DoAA" the Chairperson, Senate may approve the topic, Pending the endorsement by the Senate- k) Any change in the title of the dissertation by the Senate at any subsequent stage is permissible at the requqst to be made by a PhD scholar in the prescrib€d format (Annexure) iI such change is agreed to by the SupeNisor and endorsed by the concerned DRC Chairperson of concemed DRC shall send the application to DoAA who shall forward the same to the Chairperson, Senate for apProval. l) Dissertation topic must be finalized within four semesters from the date of registration to the PhD Programme. m) Registralion of a PhD scholar, availing semester drop, shall stand canceled if s/he fails to finalise the topic wihin three years (i.e., wiflin six consecutive sem€sters) from the date of registradon to the PhD programme' n) A PhD scholar shall get three chances to get his'her PhD topic finalized within the stipulated period as given under RG 12 (land m). o) lf the PhD scholar fails to defend his/her topic for the third time, his'/her PhO regislration shall stand annulled. Reoulation 13: Definition of sueervisor t co-Suoervisor, Extemal Guide 'SupeMsoi shall mean a permanent tulltime facutty of SPAB, holding a PhD and having expertise in tho proposed arca of research of the respeclive PhD scholar to supeNise latb/s research / academic wod( S/he will be considered as lhe main guide and shall be consulted in all matters related to the research works of the PhD scholar after his /her appointnent.'Co - SupeNisor'shall mean a permanent tutltime faculty of SPAB or any other recognized institution, holding a PhD and having epertise in the proposed area of research of the respective PhD scholar to co - supervise the research / academic work of fle PhO scholar' 'Extemal Guide' ap\oved io 3{ BoG l,llPage -- 17 of 34 -- shall mean an expert in the con(Emed rqsearch area of the respective phD scholar trom outsid€ SPAB to co - supervise the research / academic work of the phD scholar. The Co-supeNisor / Ext6mat Guide (rf any) shall undertake joint supervision of the research work of the phD scholar through mutual consultations with the Supervisor. Minimum years of experience required ior a permanent fulltime faculty of SPAB to be eligible to become Supervisor or Co-supervisor shafl b€ two years and one year respectivety from the date of award of phD degree to him / her. Requlation 14i Choice of Supervisor a) Takng into consideration the .esearch prcfle of the permanent fulltime faculty of SPAB, a phD scholar shafl give his /her choice for Supervisor in prescribed fomat after obtaining the consent of the particular faculty of SPAB. Regulation 29: Leave Admissible for a Full Time phD Scholar a) Ordina.v Leave: A tulltime PhD schotar shall be eligibte for thirty days, leave for every completed year (calc.ulated in terms of two @nsec{jtive semesters, from the time of hjs/her joining the phD programme). Saturdays, Sundays or holidays during the leave period shall be counted towards leave, except for prefxed or sufiixed holidays Awroved in 34 BOG 29lPage -- 32 of 34 -- Of the thirty days' leave, a maximum of fifreen days' of leave is permitted in a semestea However. a PhD scholar c€n accumulate leave, and avail a maximum of 30 days leave at a lime in a year' The maimum nr'lmber of canied-over leave, from one completed year to another' is fifleen days' Chairpe6on of concerned DRC shall sanction the leave on recommendaton of the SupeNisor. The ofice of DoAA shall maintain the Leave Register' A PhD scholar shall sign lhe Leave Register both before proceeding for leave and after availing the lEave. b) Matemitv/Patemitv Leave: A full-time PhD scholar shall be eligible for four months' maternity leave or fifteen days of paternity leave as the case may be and as applicable only once during the PhD Programme. Chairperson oI concemed DRc shall sanolion matemity '/ patemity leave on the recommendation of the Supervisor and on the submission of a certificate ftom a registered medical officer. The oflice of DoAA shall maintain the Matemity rl Paternity Leave Register. A PhD scholar shall sign the Matemity / Paternity Leave Register both before proceeding for leave and after availing lhe leave. c) A@lg$g-tgeyg A full-time PhD scfiolar shall be eligible ior academic leave upto a maximum ot six weeks during a calendar year on the following grounds. i.
